slack_message:
📋 *Commit Slip Report — May 2024*
3 deals committed to close in May did not close. Here's the post-mortem before your *June 4 @ 9:00am* forecast meeting.

---

*Deal 1: Acme Corporation — $85,000 | AE: Sarah Mitchell*
• *Original Close Date:* May 31 / *New Projected:* June 30
• *Root Cause:* Champion disengagement + no exec sponsor confirmed. Critical Event was rep-driven ('end of Q2'), not tied to an external forcing function.
• *Risk Rep Missed:* Last call was May 12 (19 days before month-end). Buyer hesitation flagged on that call. Rep committed to follow-up materials — never sent. No next meeting scheduled.
• *Recommended Action:* Sarah to re-engage Lisa Park this week. Require introduction to COO before re-committing to June close. Revisit Critical Event.

---

*Deal 2: Brightwave Technologies — $142,000 | AE: James Okonkwo*
• *Original Close Date:* May 31 / *New Projected:* June 28
• *Root Cause:* Economic buyer (CFO) never engaged in the process. Legal backlog used as cover for a deal that was never fully qualified at the financial decision level.
• *Risk Rep Missed:* SPICED notes listed CFO as signer but no CFO call ever occurred. Scope expanded on May 8 call (5 additional seats) with no pricing discussion — adds uncertainty to close.
• *Recommended Action:* James to schedule CFO intro call this week. Do not re-commit until CFO has reviewed commercial terms. Clarify scope and reprice if needed.

---

*⚠️ Note: Meridian Health Group ($210,000 | AE: Carla Russo) excluded from slip analysis.*
_This is a managed push — legal redlines are standard for healthcare procurement. Strong buying intent confirmed on May 28 call with all stakeholders. Expected to close first week of June._

---

*📊 Team-Level Pattern:*
2 of 2 true slips had no external forcing function in their Critical Event. Both reps used internal deadlines ('end of quarter', 'board wants it') with no buyer-confirmed urgency. This is a *forecasting discipline issue*, not a market signal — worth addressing in the forecast call as a coaching moment on Critical Event qualification.

*Metrics this month:*
• Slip rate: 16.7% (target <15%) 🔴
• SPICED gap rate: 100% of true slips had at least 1 gap 🔴
• Attention coverage: 0% of slipped deals had a call in last 14 days 🔴

📅 *Your next forecast leadership meeting is Tuesday, June 4 @ 9:00am. This report is ready as a standing agenda item.*

deals.what_rep_believed: Deal was commit based on champion (Lisa Park) verbal commitment and Q2 internal deadline.
deals.what_signals_showed: Champion disengaged on last call (May 12). Rep never followed up. No exec sponsor. Last activity 19 days before month-end.
deals.root_cause: Champion not mobilized — no executive sponsor confirmed, rep-driven Critical Event
deals.risk_rep_missed: Buyer hesitation flagged on May 12 call. Internal vendor freeze mentioned. Rep commitments not fulfilled. No next step set.
deals.recommended_action: Re-engage Lisa Park. Require COO introduction before re-committing. Redefine Critical Event.

deals.what_rep_believed: Legal review was the only blocker. Deal was otherwise solid.
deals.what_signals_showed: CFO (economic buyer) had never been in a call or meeting. Scope expanded on last call. Legal delay is symptom of incomplete qualification.
deals.root_cause: No economic buyer access — CFO required to sign but never engaged in sales process
deals.risk_rep_missed: SPICED listed CFO as signer but no CFO touchpoint ever occurred. Scope creep on May 8 call not addressed.
deals.recommended_action: CFO intro call this week. Do not re-forecast until CFO reviews terms. Reprice expanded scope.
spiced_gaps: Economic buyer listed but never engaged
call_flags: CFO never appeared in any call

managed_push.reason_excluded: Customer-initiated push with documented legal redlines. All stakeholders engaged. Strong buying intent confirmed.
